entry.title: SAC Excel Add-in - Unable to Maintain Cost Center & GL Account Row Filters from Planning Form Export
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m running into an issue with a SAC planning form that we are exporting to the SAC Excel Add-in, and I’m hoping someone has seen this before or found a workaround.

We have a planning form in SAC where users plan by Cost Center and G/L Account. In the SAC form itself, users are able to work with the hierarchy and select specific nodes or choose the members they want under those nodes.

However, once the same form is opened in the SAC Excel Add-in, the behavior changes.

The main issue is that users are not able to properly maintain the filters for Cost Center and G/L Account in Rows.

What makes this more complicated is:

  1. Cost Centers are assigned to users through security/data access control
  2. Some G/L Accounts are locked intentionally because they are planned in the Workforce model and crossed over into the Income Statement model
  3. In the SAC form, users can select specific nodes and effectively narrow down what they want to work on
  4. In the Excel Add-in, that same flexibility is not available. Instead of being able to cherry-pick members under a node, users end up getting all Cost Centers or all G/L Accounts under that selection

We are also seeing messages in the Excel Add-in related to complex filters, and in some cases the filter cannot be displayed or maintained in the filter dialog.

What I’m trying to understand is:

  • Is this a known limitation of the SAC Excel Add-in when the source table/form contains hierarchy-based or complex filters?
  • Does security on Cost Center impact how the Excel Add-in handles row filtering?
  • Is there any workaround that would still allow users to select specific Cost Centers or G/L Accounts instead of bringing in everything under the node?
  • Has anyone found a better design approach for planning forms that need to be used both in SAC and in the Excel Add-in?

Our goal is to let users work in Excel, but still keep the same controlled planning experience they have in SAC as much as possible.

Any ideas, recommendations, or workaround suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Free Goods Determination API which can be use for Dat...
entry.content:
Hi Abulhasnat,

 

Standard SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud does not provide a dedicated, direct API for bulk maintaining Free Goods Determination (VBN2). You cannot wrap a custom BAPI around it like in On-Premise.

 

You have two standard alternatives:

1. Price Management API: Map your free goods logic as a 100% discount. Use the Condition Record for Pricing in Sales API (API_SLSPRICINGCONDITIONRECORD_SRV). Your third-party system can push the condition records directly here.

2. Sales Order A2X Payload: If the external system generates the order, handle the logic externally. Pass the free item (e.g., Item Category TANN) with the 100% discount already applied when calling the Sales Order (A2X) API.

entry.title: Re: Update settlement rule of maintenance order
entry.content:
Hi there,

 

The issue with EXIT_SAPLCOI1_027 is exactly as your developer noted - it is tightly coupled to the SAP GUI and won't consistently trigger across WebDynpro or Fiori (UI5) interfaces.

 

For Maintenance Orders in modern SAP, you have two main options:

Option 1: Standard EAM Configuration - Before coding, verify if you can map the receiver directly in standard Settlement Profile configuration based on the Equipment/Functional Location category.

Option 2: The WORKORDER_UPDATE BAdI - If custom logic is mandatory, use the WORKORDER_UPDATE BAdI, specifically the AT_RELEASE or BEFORE_UPDATE methods. This operates at the database update level, ensuring it triggers regardless of whether the order was released via Fiori, GUI, or an API call.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Cloud: Trigger outbound HTTP call to Feishu /...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, you cannot directly embed an outbound HTTP REST call (like a webhook to Feishu) inside a standard Flexible Workflow step.

 

The standard architectural approach requires decoupling the event from the action:

1. Enterprise Event Enablement: Configure your S/4HANA system to emit a standard business event when the specific workflow is approved.

2. SAP BTP Integration: Subscribe to this event using SAP Event Mesh or SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration).

3. The Feishu Call: Build the integration flow in Cloud Integration to catch the event, transform the SAP JSON payload into the Feishu-compatible format, and trigger the outbound HTTP POST to the Feishu Integration Platform.

entry.title: Re: Request for Bulk Partial Payment Posting Solution SAP...
entry.content:
Hi Ankit140,

 

Let's save you some time: there is no standard, out-of-the-box Fiori app in S/4HANA Public Cloud designed specifically for bulk uploading and posting partial payments via Excel. Fiori apps like Post Incoming Payments (F1345) and Clear Incoming Payments are built for manual, individual processing. Unless you can adapt your process to rely purely on standard Electronic Bank Statement (EBS) interpretation algorithms for mass automated clearing, standard config won't cover this.

 

To achieve an Excel-driven mass partial clearing process, you are looking at custom development. Typically, you would need to build a custom Fiori application that parses your specific Excel template and orchestrates the clearing logic via the Journal Entry API (API_JOURNALENTRYITEMBASIC_SRV).

entry.title: Re: How do you handle sensitive data when integrating ext...
entry.content:
Hi there,

 

Integrating external GenAI with SAP requires strict architectural boundaries to prevent PII leakage. Standard direct API calls from S/4HANA to public LLMs (like OpenAI) are highly discouraged for enterprise data.

 

Here is the standard architectural approach to secure the data:

1. SAP Generative AI Hub (BTP): This is the primary standard path. It provides tenant isolation, enterprise data privacy agreements (where prompts are not used to train the base models), and central credential management.

2. Data Redaction Middleware: Before any S/4HANA payload hits the LLM endpoint, pass it through SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) or an external middleware layer designed to scrub PII (using regex or NER models) so the AI only receives anonymized context.

entry.title: Re: Equipment User Status Functionality in SAP S/4HANA Pu...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, defining traditional User Status Profiles (the equivalent of OIBS in On-Premise) and assigning them to Equipment categories is fully supported.

 

You can configure this via the Manage Your Solution or Central Business Configuration (CBC) app:

1. Search for the configuration activity Define User Status Profile (SSCUI 103250). Here you define your specific statuses and the business transactions they are allowed to restrict.

2. Then, use the activity Assign User Status Profile to Equipment Category (SSCUI 103248) to link the profile to your specific equipment types.

entry.title: Re: BAPI_PO_CHANGE – Custom PO Item fields not updated vi...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

The most common culprit when BAPI_PO_CHANGE ignores custom fields in EXTENSIONIN (despite correct append structures like BAPI_TE_MEPOITEM) is a missing or incomplete mapping in the standard BAdI.

 

You must implement the BAdI ME_BAPI_PO_CUST. Specifically, use the method MAP2I_EXTENSIONIN to map the custom fields from the BAPI_TE_MEPOITEM structure to the internal PO structures (like EKPO). Without this BAdI active and coded, the BAPI fundamentally does not know where to push the data from the extension container to the database, and it will silently ignore the update without throwing an error in the return table.

entry.title: Re: SAP API Restriction: Only Purchase Orders with Docume...
entry.content:
Hi there,

 

Let's save you some time: yes, this is a known and frequently encountered restriction in standard S/4HANA Public Cloud. The standard API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV OData service rigidly validates against standard document types (like NB) when processing extensibility logic via standard BO nodes.

 

If it is outright blocking or ignoring your Z* document types, you generally have to pivot away from using the standard OData service for those specific custom objects. The standard alternative is utilizing Developer Extensibility (ABAP Environment) to create a custom RAP wrapper that handles the custom document type and explicitly orchestrates the updates using EML (MODIFY ENTITIES).

entry.title: Re: Excel Upload Functionality for Payment Entries in SAP
entry.content:
Hi Indira,

 

Standard S/4HANA Public Cloud does not natively support free-text Excel uploads for incoming payments without strict formatting. The standard Fiori apps are generally designed for manual clearing or interpreting standard bank files.

 

You have two standard options to handle mass uploads:

1. Convert to Standard Bank Format: Instead of a raw Excel upload, convert your Excel data into an MT940 or CAMT.053 format via an external script. You can then use the standard Manage Bank Messages or Manage Incoming Payment Files apps to process the clearing automatically using standard EBS interpretation algorithms.

2. Custom Fiori App (API-based): Build a custom Fiori application that parses the Excel file and pushes the entries into SAP via the Journal Entry - Post API (API_JOURNALENTRYITEMBASIC_SRV).

 

The tradeoff is complexity. Converting to MT940 relies on standard SAP clearing rules but requires external file manipulation. The custom Fiori app gives you a seamless UI but introduces development overhead.

entry.title: Re: Maintain PIR app and splitting indicator in S4 Hana p...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, you will notice that the legacy R/3 plant-level splitting indicator configuration is not available in the same way. The standard Maintain PIRs app natively expects you to maintain the periods directly (e.g., Weekly) rather than relying on SAP to automatically split a monthly upload into weekly buckets post-upload.

 

To handle weekly planning without manual math, here is the standard workaround process:

1. Update Material Master: Ensure the material's forecast period indicator is set to Weekly (W) in the MRP 3 view.

2. Direct Weekly Upload: The Maintain PIRs app supports a 52-week maintenance horizon. You must format your Excel upload file to contain weekly quantities *before* you upload it to the app.

3. Use SAP IBP (Alternative): If your business strictly forecasts monthly, the standard architectural recommendation is to use SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) to generate the forecast, split it into weekly buckets, and then integrate it back to S/4HANA Public Cloud as weekly PIRs.

entry.title: Re: How do I retrieve a CSRF token form SAP Integration S...
entry.content:
Hi there,

 

To securely retrieve and use an X-CSRF-Token in SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) when calling an external OData service or an SAP backend API, you must decouple the fetch and the post into two steps.

 

Here is the exact step-by-step approach to configure this in your integration flow:

1. Create the Fetch Request: Add a Request Reply step with an HTTP/OData receiver adapter pointing to your target endpoint (or a lightweight entity). Set the method to GET.

2. Set the Request Header: In a Content Modifier *before* the GET call, add a Message Header named X-CSRF-Token and set its value to the constant string Fetch.

3. Capture the Response: When the GET call returns, the target system will include the generated token in the response headers.

4. Pass to the POST Call: In a subsequent Content Modifier, read the X-CSRF-Token from the incoming response headers and pass it as a Request Header into your actual POST/PUT HTTP receiver channel. Ensure you also pass back the Set-Cookie session cookies if the backend requires stateful validation.

entry.title: Re: Rate Limiting on SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 APIs - Must to know
entry.content:
Hi Jilian

If the limit (150,000 + 10% (if increased)) reached, no new API calls reached to SAP V2. Those are rejected. We had this issue and we started limiting the API usage.

API Usage metrics:

User Menu -> System Settings -> Usage and Statistics -> API Metrics

If you don't find then you need to reach out SAP to enable. It is enabled by client request.

entry.title: Re: Advanced intercompany STO - Sales Area Mismatch in Er...
entry.content:
Hello!

Please, follow the instructions from OSS Note 2625459 - Error Message VP210

 

Cause:

The field 'Item Division' is flagged in Customizing of sales order type in the transaction VOV8 so the system proposes the division from the material master of the item and the division of material is not the same as the division of the ship-to-party.

If this flag is switched off, then the header division is accepted as the item division automatically. If this flag is switched on, then the item division is taken from material master.

Resolution:

- either switch off the flag 'Item Division' in Customizing of sales order type in transaction VOV8 (the division of sales order will be determined from the customer settings)

- or change the division of the material in the material master in transaction MM02 on the view 'Sales Screen 1'

- or define the customer for the same sales area as the material

- or make the relevant configuration in transaction VOR2 to use the common division (customizing table TVTA)

entry.title: Re: Importing users failed. Error is: Missing required di...
entry.content:
Root Cause is Display Name setting is checked as Required. 
Uncheck the tick box 

via Applications and resources>Tennant Settings > Authentication - login Alias > Required (remove check box for Required) 

Steps to replicate issue

  1. Download for IDP from S/4 Public Cloud Maintain Business User

  2. Open Admin Console

  3. Select Import Users 

  4. Select Bundled Applications - Productive System 

  5. Browse CSV File

  6. Click Import

  7. Error Occurs

entry.title: Meet The Expert: Next-gen SAP Ariba Sourcing and SAP Ariba Contracts - Live Demo
entry.content:
We are excited to share details regarding an upcoming Live Demo on Next-gen SAP Ariba Sourcing and SAP Ariba Contracts. 

Aimed at existing SAP Ariba customers, this demo will provide a hands-on look at what’s new, what’s changing, and how to prepare for the transition. Through live product demos and real-world use cases, you’ll see exactly how Next-gen SAP Ariba differs from the classic experience, and the business value it can unlock.

We are beyond offering a feature pack or a user interface refresh.  We are bringing you a complete reengineering of SAP Ariba as an AI native platform - think fully microservices-based model that offers greater scalability and faster, lower impact innovation.  AI takes on the burden of manual processes and frees your procurement team to focus on strategic, high value business decisions. 

Join the Upcoming Session 

Date: Wednesday, July 8, 2026 

Topic: MTE - Next-gen SAP Ariba Sourcing and SAP Ariba Contracts

Time: 9:00 am – 9:30 am CDT

Language: English 

Future sessions on additional products are listed below.

Register for the event through the Spend Management Value Map Events and access the complete document on this topic here. 

To access Value Map Groups, you must login to SAP Community with an S-user ID or a Universal ID. 

Note: To provision a Universal ID, please follow these steps:  

(1) Visit the SAP Universal ID website 

(2) sign up for an account using your company email, and 

(3) link your existing S-user ID to your new Universal ID. 

 This session is relevant for our existing SAP Ariba customer team members: 

  • Developer 
  • Business User 
  • Administrator 
  • Architect

It will provide practical insights into the roadmap, architecture, and what steps you should start considering now. 

This is just the beginning. More events will continue to be populated under the Spend Management Value Map Events, so stay tuned and check back regularly to see what’s coming next. 

Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps   

Beyond this topic, SAP Enterprise Support value maps on SAP Community provides guidance, recommended actions, and supporting resources to help you decide what to focus on next—all included in your SAP cloud subscription.   

👉Watch short demo video, then head over to  our page  explore our range of topics and request a call  with a topic expert for guidance tailored to your goals.    

About the author:

Ernie Cosper is a Topic Expert for SAP Enterprise Support value maps, specializing in Spend Management. With over a decade at SAP and prior to this another decade in consulting and implementations, she brings deep experience from a range of customer-facing and managerial roles. Ernie enjoys working with customers, partners and peers to drive value and forge success from the foundation of SAP Enterprise Support.

entry.title: Trigger an SAP Build Process Automation Workflow from CAP Using the @cap-js/process Plugin
entry.content:
Overview

 

Previously, triggering an SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) workflow from a CAP application required direct REST API calls. On April 26, 2026, the Node.js plugin `@cap-js/process` was released as its first beta, offering a native CAP integration. This guide walks through the complete setup step by step.

 

Prerequisites
  • Your BTP subaccount is set up for SAP Build Process Automation. Refer to the SBPA initial setup guide
  • CAP local development tools are installed. Refer to CAP initial setup .
Procedure

 

Step 1 — Develop and Deploy a Process in SBPA
Create and deploy your process in the SAP Build Process Automation designer.
 

 
After deployment, locate the workflow instance ID in the deployment details.
 

 

In this example the process definition ID is:
ap10.iac-apj-workshop-i30jhz3m.wfcap1.wfcap1
 
Note this value — it is required in Steps 5 and 7.

 

Step 2 — Generate a Sample CAP Project
cds init capwf --nodejs --add sample
cd capwf
 

 

 

Step 3 — Add the `@cap-js/process` Plugin
npm add @cap-js/process
 

 

 

Step 4 — Bind the CAP Project to the SBPA Service Instance

 

Log in to Cloud Foundry and bind the `ProcessService` to your SBPA service instance.
cf login -a https://api.cf.<cf-data-center>.hana.ondemand.com --sso
cds bind ProcessService -2 <service-instance>

 

> Replace `<cf-data-center>` with your Cloud Foundry region (e.g. `eu10`, `us10`) and `<service-instance>` with the name of your SBPA service instance.

 

 

Step 5 — Import the Process as a CDS Service

 

Use the `process_definition_id` from [Step 1].
cds import --from process --name <process_definition_id>
 

 

 

Step 6 — Open the Project in VS Code

 

 

 

Step 7 — Annotate the CDS Service to Trigger the Workflow

 

Edit `srv/admin-service.cds` to add `@bpm.process` annotations to the `Books` entity.

 

**Before:**
using {sap.capire.bookshop as my} from '../db/schema';
service AdminService {
entity Authors as projection on my.Authors;
entity Books as projection on my.Books;
entity Genres as projection on my.Genres;
}
 

 

After:
 
 

 

 

 

Key annotations explained:
AnnotationPurpose
@BPM.process.startTriggers the SBPA workflow when the `submit` action is called, mapping entity fields to process inputs
@BPM.process.cancelCancels the running workflow instance when the entity is deleted
@BPM.process.businessKeySets the business key used to correlate the workflow instance with the CAP entity
 

 

 

Step 8 — Run the Application Locally

 

Start the application in hybrid mode so it connects to the bound SBPA service instance.
cds watch --profile hybrid
 

 

 

Step 9 — Test the `submit` Action

 

Create `test/http/AdminService.http` and send the following request to trigger the SBPA workflow for an existing active book.

 

@server=http://localhost:4004
@username=alice
@password=

### Books Submit action on an existing active Book (ID=201)
# @NAME Books_Submit_Existing
POST {{server}}/odata/v4/admin/Books(ID=201,IsActiveEntity=true)/AdminService.submit
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Basic {{username}}:{{password}}


{}

 

 

 

The terminal confirms the workflow was triggered — notice `[process] – Starting process ap10.iac-apj-workshop-i30jhz3m.wfcap1.wfcap1` and the book data passed as inputs.

 

 

Step 10 — Verify the Workflow Task in SBPA My Inbox

 

Open the SAP Build Process Automation **My Inbox**. The approval task appears with the book data (ID, author, title, description, genre, stock, price, currency) populated from the CAP entity.

 

 
The End!
 
Thank you for your time!

entry.title: Re: Material can no longer be changed
entry.content:
The Material field in a Purchase Order is subject to standard screen layout control in SAP. Once a goods receipt has been posted against the PO, the Material field becomes greyed out and can no longer be edited.
This behavior is controlled by hard-coded logic within the SAP standard program. The field status is set to * (display only) when the GR quantity in the PO history is not zero. The relevant technical location is:

Include: LMEPOF8D
Class: LCLFS_MMMFD_MATNR
Method: LIF_FIELD_SEL~EXECUTEF

For more information of screen layout behavior in Purchase Orders and Purchase Requisitions, you may refer Note 30316, KBA 2981295 and KBA 2441129.

entry.title: Re: 購買発注(ME21N)の「個人設定」の初期値が設定されてしまっている
entry.content:
Personal settings for MM purchasing transactions are stored in table ESDUS. The Purchasing Organization is not automatically updated in personal settings, it either be saved manually by the user via the Personal Settings dialog, or copied from a source user during user creation in SU01 with the Personalization flag enabled.
There is no standard change document log for parameter changes made in SU01, as user parameters (table USR05). KBA 3372787 introduces how to enable change documents for specific field of database table. The table name for user parameter is USR05.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Launchpad throwing an error "500 Internal Serve...
entry.content:
Hello,

Could you please check the following:

1.Verify ICF Services Are Active (SICF)
Ensure the following ICF nodes are active post-update:

/sap/bc/ui2/flp
/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/ui2/ushell
/sap/public/bc/ui5_ui5/
/sap/public/bc/ui2/
/sap/bc/ui2/start_up
/sap/public/bc/its (and all subnodes, if SAP GUI for HTML apps are used)

2.Clear Metadata and page Caches

Run transaction /IWFND/CACHE_CLEANUP on the Gateway system.
Run transaction /IWBEP/CACHE_CLEANUP on the Backend and Gateway.
Run report UI2/PAGE_CACHE_SYNCHRONIZE

3.Check Work Process Logs and ST22 for ABAP Dumps

Use transaction SM21 to review system logs for errors occurring at the time of the 500 error.
Use transaction ST22 to check for ABAP short dumps
Use transaction /n/IWFND/ERROR_LOG for Gateway error log

Best regards,
Joe

entry.title: TenantDBにおけるMinicheckのM1143について
entry.content:
SAP PERSON_NAME 2 SPS 08のTenantDBでSQL『HANA_Configuration_MiniChecks_2.00.080+.txt』を実施しました。
実施結果を『OSS_Minicheck.xlsx』として添付しますので、M1143について対応事項を確認させてください。

M1143についてNote.DIGITSを確認したところ詳細に下記の記載がありました。
『特定のアプリケーションソースが大量の SQL キャッシュ (動的文、大量の文、動的 IN リストまたは OR 連結長、バインド変数なしなど) を消費する理由を確認し、SQL キャッシュの使用を減らすために適切なアクションを実行します。』

分析SQL文"HANA_Memory_SQLCache_TopConsumers"を実行結果を『M1143_HANA_Memory_SQLCache_TopConsumers.txt』として添付します。

Minicheckの本警告について、対応が必要かどうかはどのように判断できますでしょうか。
分析SQL文を実行しましたが、対応要否の判断が不明です。
また対応が必要な場合、具体的な対応手順についてご教示いただけますでしょうか。

以上、よろしくお願いします。


--- Support Assistant ---

Q: Which SAP PERSON_NAME Platform Version and revision do you use (eg IP_ADDRESS)?
A: Revision IP_ADDRESS (SPS08) for PERSON_NAME DB 2.0

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: TenantDBにおけるMinicheckのM1143について
entry.content:
ご連絡いただくMinicheck　レポートでの警告項目M1142・M1143　について、
下記のノートでの通り、関連可能の既知問題が複数に記載されております。

・ノート
2124112 - FAQ: SAP HANA Parsing
-> Lack of bind variables
=>
M1142
M1143

本件の対応について、上記のノートの通り、
既知問題のチェック、問題の予防としてフィクスを適用するかの検討、になります。

また、ご連絡いただくMinicheck　レポートでの警告項目について、
システムの自動判断として問題発生した可能性があることを示していると認識されますが、
実際の環境に特に問題ないと確認できる場合、無視可能になります。

システム中に実際のパフォーマンス問題が発生していると確認できる場合、
その現象の詳細をご連絡いただければと存じます。

＊例えば、CPU高騰のパフォーマンス問題が発生する場合、現象発生の時間帯などの詳細をご連絡ください。

entry.title: Best Practice to Activate Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad for German and Austria and Italy
entry.content:
中国客户的出海项目，需要激活德国、奥地利、意大利工厂的  Registration for Indirect Taxation Abroad (RITA) 国外间接征税登记 (RITA) ，希望了解如何不影响 DRC Cloud Edition的正常使用，避免影响到DRC Cloud Edition报表的准确性。

RITA 激活时：
1. European Community（欧洲共同体）销售清单中报告货币金额可能不正确，和客户使用场景产生冲突，金额不正确会产生很大的风险
2. 若 EC 销售清单设置了"税行项目基准金额"参数，则当三角交易标识或增值税号不唯一时，货币折算不生效

三角交易处理方式流程如下：
4  Triangular Transaction (Dreiecksgeschaeft) / 三角交易处理
U3(Germany) -> U2(Austria) -> U1(Italy) direct ship. U2 invoices U3.
Tax Treatment: U1 invoices U2 w/ VAT; U2 invoices U3 (Reverse Charge); U3 receives RC invoice

报税处理流程如下：
5  Tax Declaration (March / Juni / September) / 税务申报
SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2602 supports tax declaration via DRC module (FSD_01 3.9.3.4).
VAT filing frequency: Monthly (VAT > 7,500) deadline 10th next month; Quarterly (VAT <= 7,500); Annual (VAT <= 1,000)
Supported: UStVA, Elster, Intrastat, Reverse Charge, Annual VAT

了解到如下的已知限制，希望和SAP专家讨论有何变通办法解决：

现金折扣（Cash Discount）激活时：

  • 凭证货币与公司代码货币不同，且含不同税码时，不得修改本币金额
  • 含现金折扣或税务相关付款差异的支付/清账，付款中报告货币税额与发票不能同汇率折算
  • 同一凭证中部分行项目涉及现金折扣、部分不涉及时，税额折算汇率不一致
  • 按服务日期折算（Service-Rendered Date）激活时：
  • 支付中报告货币税额与发票折算汇率不一致

RITA 激活时：

  • EC 销售清单中报告货币金额可能不正确
  • 若 EC 销售清单设置了"税行项目基准金额"参数，则当三角交易标识或增值税号不唯一时，货币折算不生效
  • 预付款（Down Payment）： 预付款与发票必须使用相同凭证/交易货币

高级公司间流程（Advanced Intercompany）：

  • 客户发票中多税条件、含现金折扣的税基等情况暂不支持
  • 通过 IDoc 过账供应商会计凭证不支持
  • 发票校验中使用加权平均汇率可能导致税额与税基不匹配
  • Central Finance： 源系统为云系统时，不得激活历史汇率功能
  • 西班牙： 高级 ICO 流程及多税码暂不支持

如有建议，盼联络  jing.zhang@boscloud.cn 和 julia.lu@sap.com，感谢帮助。

entry.title: Error DD_ABAP_LANG_VERS011: Customizing-Includes are not allowed in ABAP language version 5
entry.content: Hello SAP Community, I am currently working on an enhancement project in our SAP S/4HANA 2023 On-Premise system. I am trying to add a custom field (ZZ001) by including it in the standard structure CI_COBL. However, when I attempt to activate the structure CI_COBL, the system throws the following error: "Customizing-Includes are not allowed in ABAP language version 5" Message No. DD_ABAP_LANG_VERS011 I have verified that my custom data element and domain definitions are correct. It appears that the ABAP language version setting for this object is restricting the use of traditional Customizing Includes. Could anyone advise on the best practice for handling this in S/4HANA 2023 OP? Should I try to change the language version of CI_COBL via the Object Directory Entry (SE03/SE11)? Or is there a recommended alternative approach (e.g., using Append Structures or BAdIs) compatible with Language Version 5? Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: We need your guidance regarding Cost Center Budgeting
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

We upload the budget using a combination of Cost Center and G/L Account. However, we have observed that when posting transactions, the system validates the budget only at the Cost Center level and not at the G/L Account + Cost Center combination level.For example, if we upload separate budgets for different G/L accounts under the same Cost Center, the system still allows postings that exceed the budget of a particular G/L account, as long as the total Cost Center budget has not been exceeded. No error message is triggered for the individual G/L Account budget.Our client requires budget control at the G/L Account + Cost Center combination level, so that the system issues an error whenever the budget for a specific G/L Account within a Cost Center is exceeded.Could you please confirm whether this functionality is supported in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud? If so, could you advise on the required configuration or the recommended approach to achieve this requirement?Thank you for your support.

Best regards,

Krishnpal Singh

entry.content:
Hi  @MichaelSchneide 

GitHub Copilot doesn't invoke Joule directly. It calls the ADT MCP Server via MCP. For AI-enabled operations (such as abap_atc_apply_ai_fix), the ADT MCP Server internally invokes the SAP Joule for Developers (ABAP AI) backend services, which generate and apply the AI-based code changes before returning the results to Copilot.

entry.title: Re: SAP RDP: ES Plastic Tax for non-EU imports
entry.content: The plastic tax for non-EU import is paid at the customs, as part of the import clearance procedures. It is just not reported in the monthly form F592, because it's already paid at the customs. You cannot get refund if you haven't paid the tax first. RDP tracks the non-EU imports, so it can calculate how much can be refunded form them.

entry.title: CAD Interfaces Now Available in SAP Integrated Product Development
entry.content:
Welcome to this blog post! Effective June 15th, 2026, SAP Integrated Product Development now supports direct integration with leading MCAD authoring tools. This marks a significant milestone for product-centric industries, closing a critical gap between engineering design environments and enterprise product data management.

Stronger Product Data. Stronger Supply Chain.

With these new integrations, SAP Integrated Product Development takes a major step forward for product-centric industries. Customers can now:

  • Manage and govern product data in a single system of record
  • Directly connect MCAD authoring tools to SAP Cloud ERP, without manual re-entry or version mismatches
  • Seamlessly transition from EBOM (Engineering Bill of Materials) to MBOM (Manufacturing Bill of Materials)
  • Improve cross-functional collaboration across engineering, sourcing, and manufacturing

These capabilities strengthen the digital thread across product development and supply chain operations, helping companies accelerate innovation and improve time-to-market.

 

What does the overall architecture for CAD integration with SAP IPD look like?

 

 

Which CAD Systems Are Now Supported?

SAP Integrated Product Development now provides dedicated plug-in adapters for three major MCAD platforms, with a fourth on the way:

  • Autodesk Inventor
  • SOLIDWORKS
  • Solid Edge
  • AutoCAD (coming soon)

These CAD plug-ins directly integrate CAD structures with design items and product structures in SAP Integrated Product Development, enabling seamless data synchronization and improving overall data quality across the process.

A Closer Look at the CAD Adapter Capabilities

Each CAD adapter is delivered as a plug-in that installs within the respective CAD environment, allowing engineers to work in their preferred tools while maintaining a direct connection with SAP.  The Product Data Integration SaaS application serves as the backbone for this connectivity, integrating data from external, non-SAP authoring systems as the authoritative source of product data.

Key capabilities include:

  • Upload: Import metadata, structures, and files from external CAD authoring systems into SAP Integrated Product Development
  • Retrieve: Read metadata and product structures from SAP and download the corresponding files back to the CAD environment
  • Design item versioning: Each save action from the external CAD system creates a new design item minor version, providing full traceability
  • Status lifecycle management: Define status profiles, assign them to design item types, and manage lifecycle transitions
  • Search and visibility: Find design items using filters such as Design Number, Design Type, Status, and Version
  • Collaboration integration: Assign design item versions to collaborations for cross-functional review and approval
  • Product structure derivation: Derive product structures from uploaded CAD data for use in the Enterprise Product Structure application
  • Visualization support: Derive 3D visualization from native 3D CAD authoring files associated to product structure.
  • Configure design types: Create and maintain design types to differentiate integrations to external authoring systems and control behavior.

For full technical details, refer to the Product Data Integration Feature Scope Description.

 

Documentation and Download Links

SAP Help Portal - Admin and User Guides for each adapter:

  • Autodesk Inventor: Admin Guide | User Guide
  • SOLIDWORKS: Admin Guide | User Guide
  • Solid Edge: Admin Guide | User Guide

Software Download Center - Download plug-ins from the SAP Software Download Center (search for "PDI" or click on the links below):

  • Autodesk Inventor plug-in: Download
  • SOLIDWORKS plug-in: Download
  • Solid Edge plug-in: Download

 

Pricing and Supplement

The CAD integrations are delivered as capabilities within SAP Integrated Product Development, with each integration representing a distinct capability.  They follow a capacity unit–based consumption model:

  • Each CAD integration capability consumes a defined number of capacity units
  • Users only consume capacity units based on the specific capabilities they actively use

This model allows organizations to scale usage based on actual needs while maintaining clear visibility into consumption.

For detailed information on capacity unit allocation per capability, please refer to the product supplement.

Conclusion

The availability of CAD adapters for SAP Integrated Product Development marks a key milestone in connecting engineering design with enterprise operations. By enabling direct, bidirectional integration between leading MCAD tools and SAP Cloud ERP, organizations can manage product data within a single system of truth - reducing errors, accelerating time-to-market, and strengthening the digital thread from concept to delivery.

Key benefits include:

  • Integrate CAD tools directly with SAP Cloud ERP, eliminating data silos between engineering and operations
  • Enable end-to-end supply chain transformation starting from the design phase
  • Enhance user experience by allowing CAD users to work within their familiar tools while staying connected to SAP

Stay tuned for the release of the AutoCAD integration, and for further updates as the CAD adapter portfolio continues to expand.

 

Screenshots of the current CAD adapters

Autodesk Inventor

 

 

Solidworks

Coming Soon….

Solid Edge

Coming Soon….

More Information

Don't hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Follow me on LinkedIn and stay up to date with our latest enablement assets: https://www.linkedin.com/in/annette-garcia-lorenzo

entry.title: Re: WBS Sales Contract Position - WBS Element
entry.content:
Guten Tag @Bergj 

Vielen Dank für Ihre Frage.

Die Kontierung eines Verkaufskontrakts auf ein PSP Element wird im Standard nicht unterstützt, weder als fachlich nutzbare Kontierung auf Kopfebene noch auf Positionsebene. Dieses Verhalten ist ausdrücklich in KBA 2668268 festgehalten. Die Meldung V2509 kann erscheinen, wenn man eine Zuordnung versucht.

Das Feld PSP Element ist zwar im Migrationstemplate bzw. In der technischen Struktur der Verkaufskontraktposition vorhanden. Die API Dokumentation listet beispielsweise das Feld WBSElement in der Entität Sales Contract Item. Weil diese Technischen Feld vorhanden ist, bedeutet nicht, dass die Kontierung für jeden Verkaufsbelegtyp fachlich freigegeben ist. Die Anwendungsprüfung für Verkaufskontrakte bleibt bestehen.

Daher lässt sich das Feld auch nicht über UI Anpassung als unterstützte Kontierung im Vertrag aktivieren.

Ich empfehle folgender Prozess:

  1. Legen Sie den Verkaufskontrakt ohne PSP Element an.
  2. Erstellen Sie den späteren Kundenauftrag mit Bezug zum Verkaufskontrakt.
  3. Ordnen Sie das abrechnungsfähige PSP Element auf Positionsebene des Kundenauftrags zu.

Die Zulässigen Positionstypen und Voraussetzungen sind unter folgender Link beschrieben.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/3804ed4c209a408d8eb660a67172139a.html?locale=de-DE

Das PSP Element muss dabei als Abrechnungselement gekennzeichnet und für die verwendete Kundenauftragsposition unterstützt sein. Die Zuordnung kann auch bei Kundenaufträgen erfolgen, die mit Referenz auf einen Vorgängerbeleg Erstellt wurden.

Die genannte Funktion bei Serviceverträgen ist ein anderer fachlicher Prozess. In Serviceverträgen können PSP Elemente als Merkmale des Ergebinsobjektes zugordnet werden, wie in Serviceverträge verwalten beschrieben. Diese Funktion is nicht auf Verkaufskontrakte übertragbar.

Ein kundeneigenes Feld würde lediglich eine Referenz speichern, aber keine echte CO Kontierung oder Standardintegration erzeugen.

 

Lassen Sie mich Wissen, wenn Sie weitere Fragen haben.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 1 — Model the Board
entry.content:
namespace snakeladder;

type TurnStatus : String enum {
  Waiting;
  Playing;
  Moving;
  Blocked;
  Finished;
}

entity BoardSquares {
  key square       : Integer;
      snakeTo      : Integer;
      ladderTo     : Integer;
      isDoubleHead : Boolean default false;
}

entity GameSessions {
  key ID         : UUID;
      name       : String(100);
      createdAt  : Timestamp;
      startedAt  : Timestamp;
      finishedAt : Timestamp;
      winner     : Association to Players;
}

@flow.status: TurnStatus
entity Players {
  key ID         : UUID;
      session    : Association to GameSessions;
      name       : String(100);
      @readonly
      position   : Integer default 1;
      @readonly
      TurnStatus : TurnStatus default #Waiting;
      turnOrder  : Integer;
      lastRoll   : Integer;
}
using { snakeladder } from '../db/schema';

service GameService {
  entity GameSessions as projection on snakeladder.GameSessions;

  @flow.status: TurnStatus
  entity Players as projection on snakeladder.Players actions {
    @from: [ #Waiting ]
    @TO: #Playing
    action startTurn();

    @from: [ #Playing ]
    @TO: #Moving
    action rollDice() returns Integer;

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Waiting
    action confirmMove();

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Blocked
    action blockPlayer();

    @from: [ #Blocked ]
    @TO: $flow.previous
    action unblockPlayer();

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Finished
    action winGame();
  }

  entity BoardSquares as projection on snakeladder.BoardSquares;
}


// I kept the suggested TurnStatus enum values and did not add any new ones, because Waiting, Playing, Moving, Blocked, and Finished already cover the player lifecycle

entry.title: Re: Mass Upload option for the Physical Inventory Count
entry.content:
Dear @haja_peermohammedh7a1ec0,

Yes. For mass physical inventory count SAP provides released standard options

For BAPI-based integration the relevant Public Cloud communication scenario is SAP_COM_0107 – Physical Inventory Document Integration. SAP lists the following released BAPIs for this scenario:

  • BAPI_MATPHYSINV_COUNT – Material Physical Inventory – Enter Count
  • BAPI_MATPHYSINV_CHANGECOUNT – Material Physical Inventory – Change Count
  • BAPI_MATPHYSINV_CREATE_MULT – Material Physical Inventory – Create

More details below:

SAP Help – BAPIs/IDocs in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP Community Calling BAPIs in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Please note that for new third-party integrations, if the released OData API covers the required process the cleaner Public Cloud approach is to use the released OData API. The BAPI option remains available where it is explicitly released by SAP for the relevant communication scenario, such as SAP_COM_0107 for physical inventory document integration

If the requirement is a business-user Excel upload of physical inventory count results, the standard app is Manage Physical Inventory Count – F5430. SAP Help confirms that this app supports downloading and uploading Excel files for entering count results and that count results can be uploaded back in the Manage Physical Inventory Count app

More details below:

SAP Help – Manage Physical Inventory Count
SAP Help – Physical Inventory Process

SAP also describes the physical inventory count entry options, including barcode scanning, Excel download/upload and manual entry

More details below:

SAP Help – Preparation and Entry of Required Stock Data

As an alternative to BAPIs, SAP also provides the released OData API API_PHYSICAL_INVENTORY_DOC_SRV / Physical Inventory Document API. For count results the count can be stored or changed using PATCH on the A_PhysInventoryDocItem entity

More details below:

SAP Help – Physical Inventory Document API: Count Physical Inventory Document Items

Summary:

Use Manage Physical Inventory Count F5430 for Excel-based mass count upload. Use SAP_COM_0107 with BAPI_MATPHYSINV_COUNT / BAPI_MATPHYSINV_CHANGECOUNT for BAPI-based integration. For OData-based integration use API_PHYSICAL_INVENTORY_DOC_SRV

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: We need your guidance regarding Cost Center Budgeting
entry.content:
Good day @srimal_athukoralae797cf 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, you can achieve a separate budget control but the important point is that Cost Center Budget Availability Control performs the check at Cost Center plus G/L Account Group level.

Therefore, when several G/L accounts belong to the same account group, their budgets are pooled. A posting may exceed the amount uploaded for one G/L account without triggering an error, provided the total budget of that account group and cost center is still available. See KBA 3505874 for more details.

So, in simple terms:

  • Uploading separate budget lines by G/L account does not automatically create separate AVC limits.
  • The account group assigned in the AVC profile determines which accounts share a budget.
  • To control each G/L account separately, create a separate account group containing only that specific G/L account.

Here is a quick guide:

Create separate account groups in Manage Global Hierarchies and select type G/L Account Hierarchy.

Create separate nodes, for example:

  • Z_TRAVEL containing only G/L account 610000
  • Z_TRAINING containing only G/L account 620000
  • Z_CONSULTING containing only G/L account 630000

Keep all account groups used by one AVC profile under the same hierarchy. See KBA 3504317.

 

Maintain the AVC profile in the configuration activity Maintain Budget Availability Control Profile for Cost Centers. Open the Profile assigned to the affected cost center, Assign the separate account groups, maintain All Activity Group (++), and set the required tolerance, for example 100% with message type Error when postings must be blocked after the full budget is consumed.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bd39d476d1e34b48afed98759286efd6/b588cc2d87254bcd983d3d927559d9cb.html?locale=en-US

3519228 - Error FCO_AVC_CC013 while activating budget availability control for cost centers - SAP for Me

Then you need to activate the profile on the cost center. In Manage Cost Centers (new version),  select the cost center and choose Edit.

Make sure you verify the following:

  • Budget Availability Control Profile is assigned
  • Availability Control is On
  • Lock Commitment Updates is Off when PR and PO commitments must consume budget. See KBA 3505874 for more insights.

 

Then use Import Financial Plan Data to test the result by uploading the separate budget amounts.  

Thereafter, verify them in Cost Center Budget Report. 

Post an amount that exceeds the budget for one G/L account while another account still has available budget. The posting should blocked for exceeded G/L account because it belongs to its own account group. The unused budget of another account group is not considered.

If the requirement also includes PR and PO commitments, you must activate scope item 213 Predictive Commitments Management. See KBA 3505874 for more details.

To summarise:

  • Separate control is possible through the account-group design.
  • Create one account group per G/L account, assign those groups to AVC profile 102781, activate the profile on the cost center and test with separate budget values.
  • Accounts within the same account group will continue to share the available budget.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: EWM: Split 1 wave item for direct picking and 2 step...
entry.content:
• If you want a purely standard solution, you’ll need to adjust ERP delivery creation so that PAL and CAR are separate items.

  • If you want to keep ERP untouched, then a custom BAdI implementation in EWM for WT creation is the pragmatic way forward. You can define rules like:

    • If quantity ≥ 1 PAL → create WT for PAL direct to stage.

    • Remaining CAR → merge with other CAR wave items for two-step.

entry.title: Re: #CoE#TFAL How to Handle Mass Upload of Transactions f...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please check following workaround approaches,

  1. API-Based Upload via Supplier Invoice or Business Partner APIs The standard Supplier Invoice API (Create, Read, Release, Reverse) does not include address information fields, which are mandatory for one-time vendor invoices. As a result, this API cannot be used directly for one-time vendor invoice creation. A custom integration layer (e.g., on SAP BTP) would be needed to enrich the payload with address data before posting. For Business Partner mass creation, the API_BUSINESS_PARTNER OData API (communication scenario SAP_COM_0008) supports create, read, update, and delete operations for Business Partner, Customer, and Supplier objects. 

  2. SAP BTP Side-by-Side Extension with Custom Business Rules A side-by-side extension on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) can be used to build a custom upload app that applies business rules for data mapping and transformation before delivering posting records to S/4HANA via SAP Standard APIs. This approach preserves upgrade stability of S/4HANA while enabling tailor-made processing logic for one-time vendor scenarios. A manual upload capability can be provided first, with automated delivery added in subsequent iterations. 

  3. SAP Build Process Automation / RPA SAP Build Process Automation can be used to orchestrate the mass processing workflow and call the released Business Partner or invoice APIs. This should be treated as a governed automation layer rather than a standalone upload app. For purchase documents, RPA integration with S/4HANA (scope items 48M/6VW) is also a recognized approach for bulk creation. 

  4. "Create Supplier Invoice – Advanced" (MIRO) as a Manual Fallback For lower volumes or error correction scenarios, the "Create Supplier Invoice – Advanced" (MIRO) app is the recommended SAP workaround for posting invoices for one-time vendors when standard import apps are not supported.

Best regards,

Yong

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 1909 to SAP S/4HANA 2025 Upgrade Preparat...
entry.content:
As a PP/QM consultant preparing for an upgrade from SAP S/4HANA 1909 to 2025, your main responsibilities are to understand simplification changes, validate business processes, and ensure readiness for new innovations. You should focus on SAP Readiness Check, simplification item analysis, Fiori adoption, and regression testing of PP/QM processes.

Key Preparations Before the Upgrade

1. Understand Simplification Items
Review the Simplification Item Catalog for S/4HANA 2025. Identify changes relevant to PP (Production Planning) and QM (Quality Management):

  • PP: Changes in MRP, production versions, advanced ATP, embedded PP/DS.
  • QM: Integration with Fiori apps, inspection lot handling, defect management.

Document which existing transactions or processes will be impacted.

2. Run SAP Readiness Check
Perform the SAP Readiness Check to assess Custom code usage and compatibility, Business process relevance and Add-ons and industry solutions. This gives you a clear list of potential issues before the upgrade.

3. Custom Code & Enhancements
Work with ABAP team to analyze Z-programs, user exits, BADIs. Identify obsolete or incompatible code. Plan remediation or redesign for PP/QM-specific enhancements.

4. Data & Master Data Validation
Ensure production versions are correctly maintained (mandatory in newer releases). Validate inspection plans, characteristics, and master data in QM. Clean up obsolete or inconsistent data to avoid migration errors.

Activities During the Upgrade Project

Functional Consultant Responsibilities

Fit-to-Standard Workshops:
Compare current processes with S/4HANA 2025 standard capabilities.

Testing:
Regression testing of PP/QM processes (order creation, confirmations, inspection lots). End-to-end integration testing with MM, SD, and WM/EWM.

Fiori Enablement:
Learn and test new Fiori apps for PP/QM. Train users on role-based access and new UX.

Authorizations:
Validate PFCG roles and adjust for new apps and transactions.

Cutover Planning:
Support data migration validation. Ensure inspection lots, production orders, and quality results are migrated correctly.

entry.title: Overlapping messages during quantity conf. result in incorrect order statuses (pcnf, final cnf)
entry.content:
Hi,

Situation Description

Quantity confirmations are sent from DM to the ERP every 5 minutes.

The last confirmations get a final confirmation flag.

During the production period, the order in the ERP may be locked, causing the message in the Integration Message to fail.

 

Behavior in DM

The retry settings in the Integration Message Dashboard allow the confirmation messages to be retried multiple times.

 

Problem

Quantity confirmations that set the status to “partially confirmed” or “final confirmation” must be processed in the correct sequence.

If an order is locked in the ERP, a “Final Confirmation” may be reset in the ERP by a later “Partially Confirmed” entry (done by the retry from DM).

If no new “Final Confirmation” is then sent from the DM, the status in the ERP remains “Partially Confirmed,” which is incorrect.

 

Question

What are appropriate approaches for such dependent bookings?

 

My Approaches

Solution 1 – Workaround

Using a job in DM that periodically reads all SFC Done entries.

I simply submit a confirmation with a quantity of 0 with a final confirmation.

This ensures that the status is ultimately correct in the ERP.

Solution 2 - Asyn CPI

According to the documentation, it is possible to configure asynchronous commit for certain collaboration links/directives.

There are also sequential queues that appear to be designed specifically for this purpose.

However, asynchronous posting requires at least the “Standard” plan in the CPI. The DM, however, only comes with a “Basic_edition.”
Is this intentional - that the full range of functions can only be achieved at additional - and very high - costs on the CPI side?

How “intelligent” is the sequential asynchronous queue? Does the logic here differentiate by plant, order, or other IDs?
If everything goes into a single queue and a message is sent to the ERP due to an error, does that block all messages, even those that might come from various plants?

Solution 3 – Build Your Own Queuing Logic

This approach is more theoretical and, in my opinion, not practical.

 

Thank you very much in advance for any suggestions.

Max

SAP Digital Manufacturing

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Dataaction resultlookup
entry.content:
Hey @MK70 ,
this is how SAC's scope narrowing works inside conditionals, and it trips up a lot of people at first.


What's happening it that when you evaluate IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Dim1]="a", [d/Dim2]="b") != 0, that condition narrows the active scope for the entire THEN block to just Dim1=a, Dim2=b. Any RESULTLOOKUP you call inside that block inherits that narrowed scope unless it explicitly overrides those same dimensions. So even though your MEMBERSET said Dim3={g,h,i}, the second RESULTLOOKUP([d/Dim3]="g") still sees Dim1=a, Dim2=b in effect from the condition, it doesn't revert to the original MEMBERSET scope.


Reordering fixes it because if you move the second RESULTLOOKUP before the IF, it runs in the original unconstrained scope (the full MEMBERSET range), so it doesn't get narrowed by the condition.


A workaround that you could try is to either pull independent RESULTLOOKUP calls outside and before the IF block (cleanest), or explicitly restate your MEMBERSET scope inside the THEN block if you need to override the condition's narrowing.


It's documented behavior, just not super intuitive. Once you know it's there, it's easy to work around. You can read it here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/766b9da1890d431ca29927daee4811b4.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

entry.title: Re: Error on calling a CAP Service/OData from SAP MDK
entry.content:
Hi Govardan,

The concept of overriding Actions in JavaScript Rules (described here: https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/getting-started/mdk/development/executing-actions.html#overriding-an-action-in-javascript-rule) is the best option to pass dynamic parameters to the MDK Action. 

The first issue which I observed in your JS Code is the structure of how you are passing the parameters to your MDK CallFunction Action. You need to make sure, that the Properties {} structure follows the same structure from the CallFunction Action. In your case, it should look like the following: 

{
   "Name": "/*******SyncApp/Actions/Docket/CreateDeliveryNote_G/CreateDeliveryNote_Online.action",
   "Properties": {
      "Target": {
         "Function": {
            "Parameters": {
               "contractNo": payload.contractNo,
               "custPoNumber": payload.custPoNumber,
               "deliveryDate": payload.deliveryDate,
               "deliveryTime": payload.deliveryTime,
               "locationText": payload.locationText,
               "notes": payload.notes,
               "plantId": payload.plantId,
               "plantName": payload.plantName,
               "products": payload.products,
               "services": payload.services,
               "shipToId": payload.shipToId,
               "shipToName1": payload.shipToName1,
               "shipToName2": payload.shipToName2,
               "soldToId": payload.soldToId,
               "soldToName1": payload.soldToName1,
               "soldToName2": payload.soldToName2,
               "shotId": payload.shotId,
               "vehicleOperators": payload.vehicleOperators
            }
         }
      }
   }
}

Besides that, you are mixing async/await and Promises for asynchronous execution. Is your JS Rule markes as "async"?

Best regards,

Robin
